mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals persistent income and consumption trajectories that translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS surprises are not positive in more than 50% of out-of-sample months, or the rank correlation between PAYEMS changes and forward returns is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates the equity risk premium and depresses near-term earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed funds rate at 4.49% in Nov 2007 near restrictive territory
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a consumer-led recessionary dynamic that sharply reduces corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS declines and UNRATE rises month-over-month, S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not negative in at least 60% of cases, or the joint signal rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a profit-margin squeeze as operating leverage reverses, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In late-cycle months, S&P 500 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are positive more than 50% of the time, or the conditional Sharpe ratio does not underperform the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.3 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate over a two-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of trigger episodes, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates healthy manufacturing activity and pricing power, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following positive INDPRO changes are not positive in more than 50% of rolling 3-year windows, or rank IC is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of accelerating consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) compresses real earnings and increases discount rates simultaneously, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint rank correlation of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS with 20-day returns is not negative over a 2-year rolling window, or returns are positive in more than 50% of joint-signal episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer spending that immediately pressures revenue expectations for discretionary sectors, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month RSAFS declines are negative in fewer than 55% of out-of-sample instances.
